data.body:
**[snitch] SNITCH-CWE-367** (warning)

Race window between Unix socket bind and permission tightening. The Unix socket is created by bind_unix_listener with default umask-derived permissions (commonly 0o755/0o775) and only restricted to 0o600 a moment later. During that window, other local users may connect to the control socket and issue Requests that spawn PTY-backed agents running arbitrary commands as the server's user. The server performs no in-band authentication and relies entirely on file-system permissions for access control.

<!-- sarif-fp: snitch:primaryLocationLineHash:src/server/mod.rs:122:CWE-367 -->

**[snitch] SNITCH-CWE-367** (note)

TOCTOU between symlink check and socket removal/bind. symlink_metadata is performed, and then the path is used again (remove_file, bind) without re-checking. A local user with write access to the containing directory could, during the race window, replace the socket path with a symlink. While remove_file on Unix operates on the symlink itself and bind() does not follow symlinks for AF_UNIX, the check-then-use pattern is fragile and any future refactor that adds a path-following operation (e.g., std::fs::remove_file on a fully resolved path, fs::metadata, or a chown) would make this exploitable.

<!-- sarif-fp: snitch:primaryLocationLineHash:src/server/mod.rs:99:CWE-367 -->
